articles.title: Tour de France 2026: Olav Kooij sprints to stage five victory in Pau – as it happened
articles.description: Dutch debutant Olav Kooij won the fifth stage of the Tour de France in a chaotic sprint finish after a crash just outside the stage finish in Pau.

articles.title: How did South Africa produce an anti-African movement?
articles.description: In a country where xenophobia has a legacy, a recent well-funded iteration has emerged that feeds on post-apartheid failures and local insecurities

articles.title: Progress against cancer not shared by poorer countries, WHO report finds
articles.description: ‘Persistent’ inequities found to exist in access to prevention, diagnosis, treatment and care, annual global review says

more.title: Khayelitsha, Langa report: BOSA calls for immediate implementation of PP directives
more.description: The Public Protector found residents had been forced to endure overflowing sewage, poorly maintained housing, broken streetlights, and inadequate security at health facilities.

more.title: SAPS rejects claims that officers killed Nigerian national in custody
more.description: SAPS has dismissed social media claims that its members killed a Nigerian national in custody, calling the reports misleading.

more.title: Bold women changing South Africa’s future honoured with top entrepreneurship award
more.description: From manufacturing sanitary pads and championing women’s safety to transforming waste into luxury fashion and harnessing artificial intelligence to improve education, six South African entrepreneurs are being recognised for building businesses that are changing lives. The finalists for the 2026 Veuve Clicquot Bold Woman Award have been announced, celebrating women who have turned bold ideas [...]

more.title: When an ice lolly becomes an economic warning
more.description: Japan’s investigation into alleged price coordination among ice cream manufacturers is about more than frozen treats.

more.title: Definite links between construction mafia and political parties – Macpherson
more.description: Says institutionalising the prevention side of the fight is an important step as the disruptors do not operate in one sphere of government, and infrastructure delivery does not sit in one department.

more.title: EFF wants parly to probe NSFAS administrator’s unapproved spending spree
more.description: EFF MP Sihle Lonzi has written to Parliament’s Portfolio Committee on Higher Education spokesperson Tebogo Letsie to allow the committee to get involved in probing whether NSFAS administrator Prof. Hlengani Mathebula was spending public funds without approval by finance minister Enoch Godongwana as stipulated in law. The EFF letter comes after Sunday World broke the […]

more.title: Mixed reactions as seven ex-MECs return to power in Mpumalanga
more.description: The return of some of Mpumalanga’s most recognisable political figures to positions of influence has reopened an old debate about experience versus recycling, following the provincial government’s unveiling of new boards for two of its flagship economic agencies. The appointment of several former Mpumalanga MECs and ANC political heavyweights to the boards of the Mpumalanga […]

more.title: Refugees in Durban say they have nowhere to go
more.description: The gate to the Home Affairs centre on Che Guevara Road, Durban. Photos: Joseph Bracken. Hundreds of refugees legally in South Africa have spent a month camped outside a Home Affairs centre in Durban. They say they cannot return to their homes because of threats of violence made against them. eThekwini Mayor Cyril Xaba has [...]

more.title: Shoprite reaches 300 community food gardens across South Africa
more.description: As food prices continue to squeeze household budgets across South Africa, many communities are searching for practical ways to improve food security. One solution gaining momentum is the humble community food garden — and Shoprite has just reached a milestone that highlights its growing impact. Turning kitchen scraps into fresh produce The retailer has officially opened its 300th community food... Read more → The post Shoprite reaches 300 community food gardens across South Africa appeared first on Cape Town ETC .

more.title: Treasury tightens public purse on red-flagged municipalities
more.description: Warnings issued and ignored, leading to action ‘unprecedented in the existence of the local government system in the country’ – with 70 municipalities given 120 days to earn the release of their equitable share grants. Professor Samuel Koma from the University of Limpopo fills us in.

more.title: Ghana says xenophobia made it reject South Africa's president's visit; Pretoria disagrees
more.description: Following the repatriation of hundreds of Ghanaians due to recent anti-immigrant protests in South Africa, Ghana has postponed South African President Cyril Ramaphosa's scheduled official visit.

more.title: LIVE | U20 World Championship: Junior Boks 33-14 Wales
more.description: LIVE updates and commentary from the U20 World Championship clash between the Junior Boks and Wales.

more.title: PSL coach earmarked for Bafana job
more.description: KickOff can reveal that SAFA has earmarked one of the PSL coaches, who led his side to a Top Eight finish last season, to be part of the Bafana Bafana set-up with changes set to sweep through the senior national team.

more.title: BMW’s Midrand centre gears for next-gen EV tech
more.description: The automaker is using technology to prepare its logistics and after-sales network for the next generation of electric vehicles.

more.title: TransBridj proves South Africa’s property market is ready for digital-first bridging funding
more.description: South Africa’s first fully digitised bridging funding platform reviews the past year having facilitated millions in funding, onboarded thousands of users, and partnered with close to 100 attorney firms.
